relevant_files.role: Pattern engine with regex-based vulnerability detection
JSON pattern definitions for security vulnerabilities
Integration tests including multi-line vulnerability limitation
Test fixture with vulnerable code patterns

conventions.commits: Conventional commits with GPG signing and DCO sign-off
conventions.testing: Unit tests in same file as implementation, integration tests in separate files
conventions.linting: Clippy with deny warnings, rustfmt for formatting
conventions.error_handling: thiserror for libraries, anyhow for applications

related_issues.title: feat(security): evaluate tree-sitter for AST-based vulnerability detection
feat(security): add negative test for multi-line vulnerability detection
related_issues.relevance: Documents current limitation that tree-sitter would address

constraints: Must maintain backward compatibility with existing regex patterns
Performance should be comparable or better than current approach
test_coverage: Good unit test coverage for pattern engine, integration tests for scanner, but limited multi-line vulnerability coverage

library_findings.notes: Core parsing library with Rust bindings

approaches.name: Hybrid Approach
approaches.description: Combine tree-sitter AST analysis with existing regex patterns. Use tree-sitter for complex multi-line patterns while keeping simple regex patterns for performance.

approaches.name: Full AST Replacement
approaches.description: Replace regex pattern engine entirely with tree-sitter queries. Convert all existing patterns to tree-sitter query syntax.

approaches.name: Parallel Engine
approaches.description: Run both regex and tree-sitter engines in parallel, merge results. Allows gradual transition and comparison.

recommendation: Start with Hybrid Approach as it provides the best balance of risk and benefit. Implement tree-sitter for complex patterns while maintaining existing regex patterns for simple cases. This allows gradual migration and validation of the new approach.
